title: cytisus hirsutus subsp. hirsutissimus
intro: Cytisus hirsutus subsp. hirsutissimus, also known as the hairy broom or Scotch broom, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Cytisus hirsutus subsp. hirsutissimus are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
